claims.text: On 17 May 2026 the WHO Director-General determined that the epidemic of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the DRC and Uganda constitutes a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC).

In total, 1481 confirmed cases; 1460 from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, 20 from Uganda and one from France (linked to DRC); and 454 deaths including two from Uganda, have been reported. As of 1 July 2026, the Democratic Republic of the Congo had reported 1,460 confirmed cases including 452 deaths (case fatality ratio 30.9%) and 213 recovered; as of 2 July 2026 Uganda had reported 20 confirmed cases including two deaths and one probable case (deceased), with 16 recovered.
As of 4 July, the country's [DRC] Government has recorded 1,561 confirmed cases, including 506 deaths and 254 people recovered. More than 10,000 contacts are being monitored.

description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo declared its 17th Ebola outbreak — caused by Bundibugyo virus — on 15 May 2026, centred in Ituri Province. On 17 May 2026 the WHO Director-General declared the DRC and Uganda epidemic a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC). Confirmed cases and deaths have continued to climb into July, with a linked imported case confirmed in France.

impact.summary: As of 4 July 2026, DRC's government had recorded 1,561 confirmed cases and 506 deaths (per UN News, citing WHO), up from 1,460 cases and 452 deaths on 1 July. WHO's most recent Disease Outbreak News (DON 612, published 3 July, data through 1-2 July) additionally lists 20 confirmed cases and 2 deaths in Uganda and 1 imported case in France; those country figures have not yet been superseded by a newer report. More than 10,000 contacts are being monitored in DRC.

location.region: Ituri Province (Mongbwalu), DRC, with cases in Uganda and an imported case in France

name: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) — DR Congo & Uganda
